basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Qaṣaṣ — “The Story” — unfolds across its eighty-eight verses the full account of Moses, from the tyranny of Pharaoh who “exalted himself in the land,” slaughtering the sons of the oppressed, to the reversal in which God makes of the weak “leaders and inheritors.” The sūra opens on the mother inspired to cast her infant into the river: God tied a knot upon her heart to keep it firm, and returned the child to her “that her eye might be comforted, and that she might know that God's promise is true.” Then Moses slays the Egyptian, flees to Madyan, waters the flock of the two women, marries Shuʿayb's daughter for the price of a covenant; he hears the call in the blessed valley — “I am indeed God, the Lord of the worlds” — receives the two signs and confronts Pharaoh, who sinks into the sea. Then comes Qārūn, whose pride and treasures the earth swallows: “Do not exult, God loves not the exultant”; “seek, through what God has given you, the abode of the Hereafter, and forget not your share of this world.” The sūra is sealed on the word in which all founders and all abides: everything is perishing except His Face — kullu shayʾin hālikun illā wajhah, and to Him all returns.
themes: the heart kept firm by God
Korah and the pride of wealth

versets.ar: طسم
versets.tr: ṭā sīn mīm
versets.trad: Ṭā. Sīn. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the three letters as a spiritual cipher: the Ṭāʾ figures purity — that of the souls of the worshippers who worship none but God, of the hearts of the knowers who magnify none but Him, of the spirits of the ecstatics who love none but Him, of the secrets of the unifiers who contemplate none but Him. The Sīn tells the secret (sirr) of God with the sinners — salvation; with the obedient — degrees; with the lovers — unceasing intimate converse. And the Mīm, His grace (minna) upon all the believers: He suffices them in every moment and steadies them on the way of good.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ الْمُبِينِ
versets.tr: tilka āyātu l-kitābi l-mubīni
versets.trad: These are the verses of the clear Book.
versets.commentaire: “The clear Book” (al-kitāb al-mubīn): the Word that illumines and distinguishes. In Qushayrī's line, the Qurʾān does not merely inform — it makes manifest what was veiled, unveiling to the prepared hearts the meanings hidden beneath the letter. The Book's clarity is not that of the eye but of insight: it is luminous for whomever God has endowed with an inner sight.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ب ي ن

versets.ar: نَتْلُو عَلَيْكَ مِن نَّبَإِ مُوسَىٰ وَفِرْعَوْنَ بِالْحَقِّ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: natlū ʿalayka min nabaʾi mūsā wa-firʿawna bi-l-ḥaqqi li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: We recite to you, in all truth, of the account of Moses and Pharaoh, for a people who believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this verse the threshold of the whole sūra: to hear the story of the Beloved from the Beloved brings ease to the heart, dispels anguish, gladdens the secret and refreshes the inmost core. If the Truth repeated the account of Moses many times, it is to magnify his rank and exalt his worth, through the eloquence of the Qurʾān, and to add at each retelling a surplus of meaning. The narrative is not repetition but deepening.

mots.sens: of the account of Moses and Pharaoh

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ عَلَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَجَعَلَ أَهْلَهَا شِيَعًا يَسْتَضْعِفُ طَائِفَةً مِّنْهُمْ يُذَبِّحُ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيِي نِسَاءَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: inna firʿawna ʿalā fī l-arḍi wa-jaʿala ahlahā shiyaʿan yastaḍʿifu ṭāʾifatan minhum yudhabbiḥu abnāʾahum wa-yastaḥyī nisāʾahum innahu kāna mina l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: Pharaoh exalted himself in the land. He divided its people into factions, oppressing a group among them: he slaughtered their sons and let their daughters live. He was truly a worker of corruption.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets divine justice against human arrogance: Pharaoh swelled without right, and God brought him low by right; he tyrannized without merit, and God humbled him deservedly. He split the people, oppressed the weak, slaughtered the sons — and God did not consent that they be left to their destruction. The tyranny that believes itself all-powerful prepares its own downfall.

mots.sens: he exalted himself in the land

versets.ar: وَنُرِيدُ أَن نَّمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَنَجْعَلَهُمْ أَئِمَّةً وَنَجْعَلَهُمُ الْوَارِثِينَ
versets.tr: wa-nurīdu an namunna ʿalā lladhīna stuḍʿifū fī l-arḍi wa-najʿalahum aʾimmatan wa-najʿalahumu l-wārithīna
versets.trad: But We willed to favor those who were oppressed in the land, to make them leaders and to make them the inheritors,
versets.commentaire: To oppression answers the design of grace. Qushayrī comments: We will to bestow Our favor upon the oppressed by delivering them from their hands, to make them guides by whom the creatures are led and from whom people learn to walk the road of sincerity. And We bless their lives, so that they inherit the dwellings of those who fought them: they become guides and beacons, lords by whom one is oriented and by whose light one is guided.

versets.ar: وَنُمَكِّنَ لَهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَنُرِيَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهَامَانَ وَجُنُودَهُمَا مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَحْذَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-numakkina lahum fī l-arḍi wa-nuriya firʿawna wa-hāmāna wa-junūdahumā minhum mā kānū yaḥdharūna
versets.trad: to establish them firmly in the land, and to show Pharaoh, Hāmān and their hosts, through them, the very thing they dreaded.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the promise explicit: We shall establish them: We shall remove fear from them, grant them ease and power, and prolong their span. And We shall show Pharaoh, Hāmān and their people what they dreaded — the loss of their kingdom at their hands. The lesson: the Truth gives, even if to the creatures it seems delayed.

versets.ar: وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ ۖ فَإِذَا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِي الْيَمِّ وَلَا تَخَافِي وَلَا تَحْزَنِي ۖ إِنَّا رَادُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجَاعِلُوهُ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-awḥaynā ilā ummi mūsā an arḍiʿīhi fa-idhā khifti ʿalayhi fa-alqīhi fī l-yammi wa-lā takhāfī wa-lā taḥzanī innā rāddūhu ilayki wa-jāʿilūhu mina l-mursalīna
versets.trad: We revealed to Moses' mother: “Suckle him, and when you fear for him, cast him into the river. Do not fear, and do not grieve; We shall return him to you and make him one of the messengers.”
versets.commentaire: This revelation, says Qushayrī, was an inspiration cast into her heart (waḥy ilhām): she acted by what God placed within her, freely. And when she entrusted him to the flood, God stilled her heart, made it firm, and inspired patience in her. The promise “do not fear, do not grieve” is the signature of providence: the child torn from the mother by the river is, by that same river, returned to her.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ي م م

versets.ar: فَالْتَقَطَهُ آلُ فِرْعَوْنَ لِيَكُونَ لَهُمْ عَدُوًّا وَحَزَنًا ۗ إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهَامَانَ وَجُنُودَهُمَا كَانُوا خَاطِئِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ltaqaṭahu ālu firʿawna li-yakūna lahum ʿaduwwan wa-ḥazanan inna firʿawna wa-hāmāna wa-junūdahumā kānū khāṭiʾīna
versets.trad: The household of Pharaoh picked him up, that he might one day be for them an enemy and a sorrow. Pharaoh, Hāmān and their hosts were indeed sinners.
versets.commentaire: The story tells the irony of the decree: those who killed the sons for fear of an enemy gather up with their own hands the very one who will be their enemy. Qushayrī recalls that destinies are not to be overcome: Pharaoh raises in his own bosom the child of his ruin, and no calculation thwarts what God has traced. The hand that thinks to seize is seized.

mots.racine: ع د و / ح ز ن

versets.ar: وَقَالَتِ امْرَأَتُ فِرْعَوْنَ قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ لِّي وَلَكَ ۖ لَا تَقْتُلُوهُ عَسَىٰ أَن يَنفَعَنَا أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُ وَلَدًا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālati mraʾatu firʿawna qurratu ʿaynin lī wa-laka lā taqtulūhu ʿasā an yanfaʿanā aw nattakhidhahu waladan wa-hum lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: Pharaoh's wife said: “A comfort of the eye for me and for you; do not kill him! Perhaps he will benefit us, or we will take him as a son.” And they did not perceive.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links this to the word “I cast upon you a love from Me”: God created in Moses' eyes such a grace that no gaze could fall upon him without being seized with love. Pharaoh's wife says “comfort of our eye” though they were childless — and they did not perceive to what his affair would lead: the love they feel is the very instrument by which God saves His messenger.

mots.racine: ق ر ر / ع ي ن

versets.ar: وَأَصْبَحَ فُؤَادُ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰ فَارِغًا ۖ إِن كَادَتْ لَتُبْدِي بِهِ لَوْلَا أَن رَّبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قَلْبِهَا لِتَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-aṣbaḥa fuʾādu ummi mūsā fārighan in kādat la-tubdī bihi lawlā an rabaṭnā ʿalā qalbihā li-takūna mina l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: The heart of Moses' mother became void; she almost disclosed him, had We not bound fast her heart, that she might remain among the believers.
versets.commentaire: Here is one of the sūra's pearls. Qushayrī: when she had cast him into the water, God stilled her heart, bound it fast, and inspired patience in her. Her inmost was void of all but the child, and she almost betrayed the secret out of human frailty — but God tied a knot upon her heart. The verb rabaṭnā (“We bound”) names the divine hand that holds the heart firm where the human alone would give way.

mots.racine: ف أ د / أ م م

mots.def: fārigh: emptied of all but him.

versets.ar: وَقَالَتْ لِأُخْتِهِ قُصِّيهِ ۖ فَبَصُرَتْ بِهِ عَن جُنُبٍ وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālat li-ukhtihi quṣṣīhi fa-baṣurat bihi ʿan junubin wa-hum lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: She said to his sister: “Follow him!” So she watched him from afar, while they did not perceive.
versets.commentaire: The mother, says Qushayrī, ordered the sister to follow his trace and to see to what his affair would lead. The sister watches him “from afar” (ʿan junub) without being detected. While providence accomplishes its design, human eyes perceive nothing: they did not sense that this watchful girl was the thread by which God would return Moses to the breast that had nursed him.

versets.ar: وَحَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِ الْمَرَاضِعَ مِن قَبْلُ فَقَالَتْ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ يَكْفُلُونَهُ لَكُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُ نَاصِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḥarramnā ʿalayhi l-marāḍiʿa min qablu fa-qālat hal adullukum ʿalā ahli baytin yakfulūnahu lakum wa-hum lahu nāṣiḥūna
versets.trad: We had forbidden him the breasts of wet-nurses beforehand. So she said: “Shall I point you to a household that will take charge of him for you and be devoted to him?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the reversal: Moses refused the breast of every woman offered him; and those who in the morning sought how to kill him, by evening exhausted themselves seeking how to feed him! When their effort failed, the sister pointed them to “a household that will take charge of him.” The refusal God placed in the child is the magnet drawing him to his mother: providence shuts every door but His.

versets.ar: فَرَدَدْنَاهُ إِلَىٰ أُمِّهِ كَيْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ وَلِتَعْلَمَ أَنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-radadnāhu ilā ummihi kay taqarra ʿaynuhā wa-lā taḥzana wa-li-taʿlama anna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: So We returned him to his mother, that her eye might be comforted and she not grieve, and that she might know that God's promise is true. But most people do not know.
versets.commentaire: The fulfillment of the word of verse 7. Qushayrī: when his mother took him back, she knew that God had verified her expectation, and her heart settled from all distress. “That her eye might be comforted”: the promised consolation returns to the letter. God's promise is true even when it passes through the river and through terror — but most people do not know how to recognize divine faithfulness beneath the trial.

mots.racine: ق ر ر / ع ي ن
و ع د / ح ق ق

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ وَاسْتَوَىٰ آتَيْنَاهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā balagha ashuddahu wa-stawā ātaynāhu ḥukman wa-ʿilman wa-kadhālika najzī l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: When he reached his full strength and maturity, We gave him wisdom and knowledge. Thus do We reward the doers of good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when his age was complete, his reason perfect and his qualities accomplished, We gave him judgment (ḥukm): We brought his attainment to completion, lavished knowledge upon him — and so has Our custom run with the great ones and the prophets. The gift of wisdom is not the conquest of man but the largesse of the Truth, granted to whom He prepares for good.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ش د د
ح ك م / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَدَخَلَ الْمَدِينَةَ عَلَىٰ حِينِ غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ أَهْلِهَا فَوَجَدَ فِيهَا رَجُلَيْنِ يَقْتَتِلَانِ هَٰذَا مِن شِيعَتِهِ وَهَٰذَا مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ ۖ فَاسْتَغَاثَهُ الَّذِي مِن شِيعَتِهِ عَلَى الَّذِي مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ فَوَكَزَهُ مُوسَىٰ فَقَضَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ۖ قَالَ هَٰذَا مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ ۖ إِنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ مُّضِلٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-dakhala l-madīnata ʿalā ḥīni ghaflatin min ahlihā fa-wajada fīhā rajulayni yaqtatilāni hādhā min shīʿatihi wa-hādhā min ʿaduwwihi fa-staghāthahu lladhī min shīʿatihi ʿalā lladhī min ʿaduwwihi fa-wakazahu mūsā fa-qaḍā ʿalayhi qāla hādhā min ʿamali sh-shayṭāni innahu ʿaduwwun muḍillun mubīnun
versets.trad: He entered the city at a time of heedlessness on the part of its people, and found there two men fighting: one of his own faction, the other of his enemies. The one of his faction sought his aid against the one of his enemies, so Moses struck him with his fist and finished him. He said: “This is the work of Satan: he is truly a misleading, manifest enemy.”
versets.commentaire: Moses meant only to repel the assailant; the Egyptian died of a single blow. Qushayrī reflects: he did not attribute the killing to Satan, but he attributed to Satan having driven him to that vehemence. And he unveils the law of causes: when God wills a thing, He sets its causes running to attain His aim; had He not willed to test Moses, that man's soul would not have been taken by such a blow — for one may receive many blows and lashes and not die. Death by a single blow was the carrying-out of a decree.

mots.sens: he struck him with his fist

mots.racine: ع د و / ض ل ل

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَغَفَرَ لَهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi innī ẓalamtu nafsī fa-ghfir lī fa-ghafara lahu innahu huwa l-ghafūru r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord! I have wronged myself; forgive me!” So He forgave him. He is indeed the Forgiving, the Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Moses repented of what had come to pass by his hand and implored his Lord. Qushayrī notes with finesse: God informed him that He had forgiven him — and there is no reproach after forgiveness. The servant, once absolved, need no longer bear the weight of blame: mercy effaces, it does not dwell on the fault.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ بِمَا أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ فَلَنْ أَكُونَ ظَهِيرًا لِّلْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi bimā anʿamta ʿalayya fa-lan akūna ẓahīran li-l-mujrimīna
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord! By the favor You have bestowed on me, I shall never be a helper to the criminals.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the favor invoked as the grace of repentance God granted him: “by what You have bestowed on me — granting me to repent — never again shall I return to what was.” True gratitude is not word but resolve: to turn from the wrong alloy and lend no more support to the guilty.

mots.sens: by the favor You bestowed on me

versets.ar: فَأَصْبَحَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ خَائِفًا يَتَرَقَّبُ فَإِذَا الَّذِي اسْتَنصَرَهُ بِالْأَمْسِ يَسْتَصْرِخُهُ ۚ قَالَ لَهُ مُوسَىٰ إِنَّكَ لَغَوِيٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: fa-aṣbaḥa fī l-madīnati khāʾifan yataraqqabu fa-idhā lladhī stanṣarahu bi-l-amsi yastaṣrikhuhu qāla lahu mūsā innaka la-ghawiyyun mubīnun
versets.trad: So he passed the morning in the city, fearful and watchful, when behold, the one who had sought his help the day before cried out to him for aid. Moses said to him: “You are clearly a man gone astray!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the senses of “watchful” (yataraqqabu): he was watching lest Pharaoh learn of the killing; or he was fearful before God of what had passed by his hand; or he feared for his people a punishment. And yataraqqabu is also to await the help of God, or to look out for a familiar soul to find solace in. The servant's fear, even mixed with remorse, remains stretched toward help from on high.

mots.sens: cried out to him for aid

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا أَنْ أَرَادَ أَن يَبْطِشَ بِالَّذِي هُوَ عَدُوٌّ لَّهُمَا قَالَ يَا مُوسَىٰ أَتُرِيدُ أَن تَقْتُلَنِي كَمَا قَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا بِالْأَمْسِ ۖ إِن تُرِيدُ إِلَّا أَن تَكُونَ جَبَّارًا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا تُرِيدُ أَن تَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā an arāda an yabṭisha bi-lladhī huwa ʿaduwwun lahumā qāla yā mūsā a-turīdu an taqtulanī kamā qatalta nafsan bi-l-amsi in turīdu illā an takūna jabbāran fī l-arḍi wa-mā turīdu an takūna mina l-muṣliḥīna
versets.trad: But when he was about to seize the one who was an enemy to them both, the man said: “O Moses! Do you want to kill me as you killed a soul yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land, and you do not want to be of those who set things right.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī clarifies the scene: this man did not know that it was Moses who had killed the day before; but seeing Moses move to stop him, he inferred that he was the killer. His words spread the matter through people's mouths — and Moses held back his hand. The secret God had veiled is unveiled at the appointed moment, setting in motion the providential exile.

mots.sens: do you want to kill me

mots.def: muṣliḥ: one who works for good.

versets.ar: وَجَاءَ رَجُلٌ مِّنْ أَقْصَى الْمَدِينَةِ يَسْعَىٰ قَالَ يَا مُوسَىٰ إِنَّ الْمَلَأَ يَأْتَمِرُونَ بِكَ لِيَقْتُلُوكَ فَاخْرُجْ إِنِّي لَكَ مِنَ النَّاصِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾa rajulun min aqṣā l-madīnati yasʿā qāla yā mūsā inna l-malaʾa yaʾtamirūna bika li-yaqtulūka fa-khruj innī laka mina n-nāṣiḥīna
versets.trad: A man came running from the far end of the city. He said: “O Moses! The notables are conferring about you to kill you. Get out! I am one who gives you sincere counsel.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: an Israelite of Moses' acquaintances came in haste: “The people want to kill you, I know their plan, they mean to inform Pharaoh — leave this city, I am a sincere counselor to you.” Providence sends its messenger at the moment of peril: Moses' going forth, which seems flight, is the opening of the road toward Madyan and revelation.

mots.sens: from the far end of the city

versets.ar: فَخَرَجَ مِنْهَا خَائِفًا يَتَرَقَّبُ ۖ قَالَ رَبِّ نَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kharaja minhā khāʾifan yataraqqabu qāla rabbi najjinī mina l-qawmi ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: So he left it, fearful and watchful. He said: “My Lord, deliver me from the wrongdoing people!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he left Egypt fearing that his trail would be followed, watchful lest his pursuers overtake him; and it is also said: watching for God's sufficiency and help. His prayer “deliver me from the unjust people” is the cry of the weak who has no recourse but his Lord — and it is precisely this destitution that opens the way of deliverance.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا تَوَجَّهَ تِلْقَاءَ مَدْيَنَ قَالَ عَسَىٰ رَبِّي أَن يَهْدِيَنِي سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ
versets.tr: wa-lammā tawajjaha tilqāʾa madyana qāla ʿasā rabbī an yahdiyanī sawāʾa s-sabīli
versets.trad: And as he turned toward Madyan, he said: “Perhaps my Lord will guide me to the right way…”
versets.commentaire: A verse dear to the Sufis. Qushayrī: he turned toward Madyan without having fixed Madyan or any other goal as his aim; he set out at adventure (ʿalā l-futūḥ), turning his heart toward his Lord, awaiting that He guide him to what is best for him. This is the journey of pure trust (tawakkul): to aim at nothing of one's own, and to let the Truth trace the road. “Such is the journey of the great ones.”

mots.racine: س و ي / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا وَرَدَ مَاءَ مَدْيَنَ وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ أُمَّةً مِّنَ النَّاسِ يَسْقُونَ وَوَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمُ امْرَأَتَيْنِ تَذُودَانِ ۖ قَالَ مَا خَطْبُكُمَا ۖ قَالَتَا لَا نَسْقِي حَتَّىٰ يُصْدِرَ الرِّعَاءُ ۖ وَأَبُونَا شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lammā warada māʾa madyana wajada ʿalayhi ummatan mina n-nāsi yasqūna wa-wajada min dūnihimu mraʾatayni tadhūdāni qāla mā khaṭbukumā qālatā lā nasqī ḥattā yuṣdira r-riʿāʾu wa-abūnā shaykhun kabīrun
versets.trad: When he arrived at the water of Madyan, he found there a group of people watering their flocks, and he found apart from them two women holding back their flock. He said: “What is your case?” They said: “We do not water until the shepherds drive off, and our father is a very old man.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a double reading: outwardly he came to the water of Madyan; but with his heart he came to the springs of intimacy and spirit. And these springs differ: those of the hearts are the gardens of expansion through unveilings; those of the spirits, the theaters of contemplation; those of the secrets, the courts of union — annihilation in the Eternal, total extinction. Moved with pity for the two women's weakness, Moses asks after them: whoever attends to the plight of the weak is bound to relieve them.

mots.sens: he arrived at the water of Madyan

mots.racine: ش ي خ / ك ب ر

versets.ar: فَسَقَىٰ لَهُمَا ثُمَّ تَوَلَّىٰ إِلَى الظِّلِّ فَقَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي لِمَا أَنزَلْتَ إِلَيَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ
versets.tr: fa-saqā lahumā thumma tawallā ilā ẓ-ẓilli fa-qāla rabbi innī limā anzalta ilayya min khayrin faqīrun
versets.trad: So he watered for them, then withdrew to the shade. He said: “My Lord, I am in need of whatever good You may send down to me!”
versets.commentaire: One of the most beautiful spiritual allusions. Qushayrī: hunger had seized him on the journey; he had never known wandering or exile, and had no money — he prayed: “My Lord, I am poor of any good You would send down to me.” And he deepens it: he withdrew to the shade of intimacy, the breath of expansion — and Moses is said to have prayed: “increase me in poverty, for my poverty toward You makes my reliance on You necessary.” To show one's weakness is the very mark of servanthood.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ظ ل ل

mots.def: faqīr: in need, poor before God.

versets.ar: فَجَاءَتْهُ إِحْدَاهُمَا تَمْشِي عَلَى اسْتِحْيَاءٍ قَالَتْ إِنَّ أَبِي يَدْعُوكَ لِيَجْزِيَكَ أَجْرَ مَا سَقَيْتَ لَنَا ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُ وَقَصَّ عَلَيْهِ الْقَصَصَ قَالَ لَا تَخَفْ ۖ نَجَوْتَ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-jāʾathu iḥdāhumā tamshī ʿalā stiḥyāʾin qālat inna abī yadʿūka li-yajziyaka ajra mā saqayta lanā fa-lammā jāʾahu wa-qaṣṣa ʿalayhi l-qaṣaṣa qāla lā takhaf najawta mina l-qawmi ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: One of the two women came to him, walking shyly. She said: “My father invites you, to reward you for having watered for us.” When he came to him and recounted to him the story, the old man said: “Do not fear! You have escaped the wrongdoing people.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the modesty (istiḥyāʾ) of the young woman: she walked in reserve because she was addressing a man not closely related to her; or: the generous one feels shy to invite. He also notes the delicacy of the verse: “when he came and recounted to him the story” — it does not say “he set out the meal” but “he recounted the story.” And Shuʿayb's words “do not fear, you have escaped”: the exhausted traveler at last finds the refuge God had reserved for him.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / أ ج ر

versets.ar: قَالَتْ إِحْدَاهُمَا يَا أَبَتِ اسْتَأْجِرْهُ ۖ إِنَّ خَيْرَ مَنِ اسْتَأْجَرْتَ الْقَوِيُّ الْأَمِينُ
versets.tr: qālat iḥdāhumā yā abati staʾjirhu inna khayra mani staʾjarta l-qawiyyu l-amīnu
versets.trad: One of the two women said: “O my father! Hire him! The best you can hire is the strong, the trustworthy.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī relates the dialogue: when Shuʿayb asked how she knew Moses' integrity, she answered: “I was walking ahead of him; he had me go behind, saying: walk behind me and point out the road, so that my gaze may not fall upon you.” Thus trustworthiness (amāna) is revealed in the mastery of the gaze. The two qualities “strong and trustworthy” are those of the accomplished servant: strength in service, modesty in guard.

mots.def: istaʾjara: to take on for wages.

mots.racine: ق و ي / أ م ن

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُنكِحَكَ إِحْدَى ابْنَتَيَّ هَاتَيْنِ عَلَىٰ أَن تَأْجُرَنِي ثَمَانِيَ حِجَجٍ ۖ فَإِنْ أَتْمَمْتَ عَشْرًا فَمِنْ عِندِكَ ۖ وَمَا أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَشُقَّ عَلَيْكَ ۚ سَتَجِدُنِي إِن شَاءَ اللَّهُ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: qāla innī urīdu an unkiḥaka iḥdā bnatayya hātayni ʿalā an taʾjuranī thamāniya ḥijajin fa-in atmamta ʿashran fa-min ʿindika wa-mā urīdu an ashuqqa ʿalayka sa-tajidunī in shāʾa llāhu mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: He said: “I wish to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine, on condition that you serve me eight years; and if you complete ten, that is up to you. I do not wish to burden you. You will find me, God willing, among the righteous.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī adds the account of the staff: Shuʿayb told Moses to enter a dark room and bring out a staff; he brought out the very one through which God would manifest his miracles — said to have been Adam's, passed from prophet to prophet. Each time Moses put it back and took another, only that staff returned to his hand; so Shuʿayb knew Moses had a rank. The covenant of labor and marriage seals, beneath an ordinary appearance, a hidden election.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ح ج ج

versets.ar: قَالَ ذَٰلِكَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ ۖ أَيَّمَا الْأَجَلَيْنِ قَضَيْتُ فَلَا عُدْوَانَ عَلَيَّ ۖ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ
versets.tr: qāla dhālika baynī wa-baynaka ayyamā l-ajalayni qaḍaytu fa-lā ʿudwāna ʿalayya wa-llāhu ʿalā mā naqūlu wakīlun
versets.trad: Moses said: “That is settled between me and you. Whichever of the two terms I fulfill, there shall be no injustice against me. And God is guarantor of what we say.”
versets.commentaire: The covenant is concluded under the gaze of the Truth. “God is guarantor (wakīl) of what we say”: the pact between men holds only insofar as it is sealed in His presence. In Qushayrī's spirit, this is to make God the witness and surety of the given word — the servant pledges himself not first to man, but to the One who watches over every oath.

mots.sens: that is between you and me

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ مُوسَى الْأَجَلَ وَسَارَ بِأَهْلِهِ آنَسَ مِن جَانِبِ الطُّورِ نَارًا قَالَ لِأَهْلِهِ امْكُثُوا إِنِّي آنَسْتُ نَارًا لَّعَلِّي آتِيكُم مِّنْهَا بِخَبَرٍ أَوْ جَذْوَةٍ مِّنَ النَّارِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَصْطَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā qaḍā mūsā l-ajala wa-sāra bi-ahlihi ānasa min jānibi ṭ-ṭūri nāran qāla li-ahlihi mkuthū innī ānastu nāran laʿallī ātīkum minhā bi-khabarin aw jadhwatin mina n-nāri laʿallakum taṣṭalūna
versets.trad: When Moses had completed the term and was journeying with his family, he glimpsed a fire on the side of the Mount. He said to his family: “Stay here! I have glimpsed a fire. Perhaps I will bring you news from it, or a brand from the fire, that you may warm yourselves.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils providence beneath trial: his wife being seized with the pangs of childbirth, the moment grew hard upon him; and it was then he glimpsed a fire on the side of the Mount. He first thought it a further trial, but it was in reality the cause of the fulfillment of his prophethood. And the loveliest allusion: He first showed him a fire, then let a light shine for him, then appeared what appeared — the aim was neither the fire nor the light, but to hear the call: “I am indeed God, the Lord of the worlds.”

mots.racine: ق ض ي / أ ج ل
أ ن س / ن و ر

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا أَتَاهَا نُودِيَ مِن شَاطِئِ الْوَادِ الْأَيْمَنِ فِي الْبُقْعَةِ الْمُبَارَكَةِ مِنَ الشَّجَرَةِ أَن يَا مُوسَىٰ إِنِّي أَنَا اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā atāhā nūdiya min shāṭiʾi l-wādi l-aymani fī l-buqʿati l-mubārakati mina sh-shajarati an yā mūsā innī anā llāhu rabbu l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: When he came to it, a call came from the right bank of the valley, in the blessed plain, from the tree: “O Moses! I am indeed God, the Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: The moment of election. Qushayrī: He veiled the precise place from conjecture — from the right bank of the valley, in the blessed plain, from the tree. And he meditates the grace of the place: there he heard the address of his Master without intermediary, and the most precious of the world's places is the theater of the beloved. On the tree: what a difference between Adam's tree, beside which his trial appeared, and Moses' tree, beside which his prophethood opened! It is the tree of union, whose fruit is nearness, its root in the soil of love, its crown high in the heaven of election.

mots.racine: ش ط أ / ي م ن
mots.sens: the right bank of the valley

mots.racine: ب ق ع / ب ر ك
ر ب ب / ع ل م
mots.def: rabb al-ʿālamīn: Master of the universes.

versets.ar: وَأَنْ أَلْقِ عَصَاكَ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَآهَا تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّهَا جَانٌّ وَلَّىٰ مُدْبِرًا وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ ۚ يَا مُوسَىٰ أَقْبِلْ وَلَا تَخَفْ ۖ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الْآمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-an alqi ʿaṣāka fa-lammā raʾāhā tahtazzu ka-annahā jānnun wallā mudbiran wa-lam yuʿaqqib yā mūsā aqbil wa-lā takhaf innaka mina l-āminīna
versets.trad: And cast your staff!” But when he saw it writhing as if it were a serpent, he turned back in flight and did not look round: “O Moses! Come forward and do not fear; you are among the secure.
versets.commentaire: When the staff became a serpent, Moses fled. Qushayrī has the Truth say: “Do not fear, O Moses: He who can turn the staff into a serpent can make of it for you a safety.” And he unveils the hidden sense: “It is not you who are meant; I established this only to unleash it upon your enemy: it is your miracle before your people, your sign against your adversary.” The passing terror is the price of an offered intimacy: “you are among the secure.”

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ع ص و
ه ز ز / ج ن ن
و ل ي / د ب ر

versets.ar: اسْلُكْ يَدَكَ فِي جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَاءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوءٍ وَاضْمُمْ إِلَيْكَ جَنَاحَكَ مِنَ الرَّهْبِ ۖ فَذَانِكَ بُرْهَانَانِ مِن رَّبِّكَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَئِهِ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا قَوْمًا فَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: usluk yadaka fī jaybika takhruj bayḍāʾa min ghayri sūʾin wa-ḍmum ilayka janāḥaka mina r-rahbi fa-dhānika burhānāni min rabbika ilā firʿawna wa-malaʾihi innahum kānū qawman fāsiqīna
versets.trad: Put your hand into your bosom; it will come out white, without blemish. And draw your arm close to you against fear. These are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his notables. They are indeed a depraved people.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this verse a lesson in action: God tells Moses to slip in his hand and cast his staff, “without your striking with it, without your using it: the affair is by Us, not by you; and it is I, not you.” Then this sublime word: “O Moses, it is in the description of your lowliness that you find Me, and in disavowing your own strength and power that you reach Me.” The two proofs shine when the hand empties itself of all pretension.

mots.racine: س ل ك / ج ي ب
mots.sens: put your hand in your bosom

mots.racine: ب ي ض / س و أ
ض م م / ج ن ح

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي قَتَلْتُ مِنْهُمْ نَفْسًا فَأَخَافُ أَن يَقْتُلُونِ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi innī qataltu minhum nafsan fa-akhāfu an yaqtulūni
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord! I have killed one of them, and I fear they will kill me.
versets.commentaire: Moses, says Qushayrī, sought every pretext, hoping to be spared the hardship of conveyance and the suffering of trial, for he knew prophethood bears its burden; but he found neither dispensation nor exemption from what he was charged with. The servant's fear is legitimate, but the decree of mission is not revoked: God does not lighten the burden, He strengthens the one who bears it.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ف س

versets.ar: وَأَخِي هَارُونُ هُوَ أَفْصَحُ مِنِّي لِسَانًا فَأَرْسِلْهُ مَعِيَ رِدْءًا يُصَدِّقُنِي ۖ إِنِّي أَخَافُ أَن يُكَذِّبُونِ
versets.tr: wa-akhī hārūnu huwa afṣaḥu minnī lisānan fa-arsilhu maʿiya ridʾan yuṣaddiqunī innī akhāfu an yukadhdhibūni
versets.trad: And my brother Aaron is more eloquent than I in speech; send him with me as a support to confirm me; I fear they will call me a liar.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God granted his request concerning his brother, where he asked that He give him a support (ridʾ), and He guaranteed them both help. The prophet, despite his strength, asks for a brotherly support: the mission does not humble one who acknowledges his need of another, for even the elect lean upon one another by the divine will.

mots.racine: ف ص ح / ل س ن

versets.ar: قَالَ سَنَشُدُّ عَضُدَكَ بِأَخِيكَ وَنَجْعَلُ لَكُمَا سُلْطَانًا فَلَا يَصِلُونَ إِلَيْكُمَا ۚ بِآيَاتِنَا أَنتُمَا وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَكُمَا الْغَالِبُونَ
versets.tr: qāla sa-nashuddu ʿaḍudaka bi-akhīka wa-najʿalu lakumā sulṭānan fa-lā yaṣilūna ilaykumā bi-āyātinā antumā wa-mani ttabaʿakumā l-ghālibūna
versets.trad: He said: “We shall strengthen your arm through your brother and give you both an authority, so that they cannot reach you. By Our signs, you two and those who follow you shall be the victors.”
versets.commentaire: “We shall strengthen your arm through your brother”: the divine answer to fear. In Qushayrī's line, God does not remove the obstacle but gives the strength to surmount it; He guarantees the two brothers an authority (sulṭān) beyond reach. The promised victory extends to “those who follow you”: whoever clings to the bearers of the sign enters the company of the victors.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ض د

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُم مُّوسَىٰ بِآيَاتِنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ قَالُوا مَا هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّفْتَرًى وَمَا سَمِعْنَا بِهَٰذَا فِي آبَائِنَا الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāʾahum mūsā bi-āyātinā bayyinātin qālū mā hādhā illā siḥrun muftaran wa-mā samiʿnā bi-hādhā fī ābāʾinā l-awwalīna
versets.trad: When Moses came to them with Our clear signs, they said: “This is nothing but fabricated magic, and we never heard of this among our forefathers of old.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the two brothers came to Pharaoh, he met them with denial and rejection, accusing them of error, falsehood and magic; they answered him with proof and called him to the clear path — but he persisted in denial. The argument of “our forefathers” is the refuge of one who refuses the newness of truth: the closed heart holds the unheard-of to be imposture.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ب ي ن
س ح ر / ف ر ي

versets.ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ رَبِّي أَعْلَمُ بِمَن جَاءَ بِالْهُدَىٰ مِنْ عِندِهِ وَمَن تَكُونُ لَهُ عَاقِبَةُ الدَّارِ ۖ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla mūsā rabbī aʿlamu bi-man jāʾa bi-l-hudā min ʿindihi wa-man takūnu lahu ʿāqibatu d-dāri innahu lā yufliḥu ẓ-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: Moses said: “My Lord knows best who comes with guidance from Him and to whom the final Abode shall belong. The wrongdoers will surely not prosper.”
versets.commentaire: Moses entrusts the judgment to the One who knows: “My Lord knows best who brings guidance and to whom belongs the happy end of the Abode.” In Qushayrī's spirit, the bearer of truth need not justify himself before the deniers — he commits the matter to God's knowledge, certain that the wrongdoers shall never know triumph. The outcome unveils what proofs could not impose.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ه د ي
ع ق ب / د و ر
ف ل ح / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يَا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَأُ مَا عَلِمْتُ لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرِي فَأَوْقِدْ لِي يَا هَامَانُ عَلَى الطِّينِ فَاجْعَل لِّي صَرْحًا لَّعَلِّي أَطَّلِعُ إِلَىٰ إِلَٰهِ مُوسَىٰ وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّهُ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla firʿawnu yā ayyuhā l-malaʾu mā ʿalimtu lakum min ilāhin ghayrī fa-awqid lī yā hāmānu ʿalā ṭ-ṭīni fa-jʿal lī ṣarḥan laʿallī aṭṭaliʿu ilā ilāhi mūsā wa-innī la-aẓunnuhu mina l-kādhibīna
versets.trad: Pharaoh said: “O notables! I know of no god for you other than myself! O Hāmān! Kindle for me a fire over the clay, then build me a tower, that I may climb up to the God of Moses — though I think he is a liar.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he claimed sole divinity, surpassing in error the idol-worshippers, who at least gave God associates. And his tower reveals his blunder: he imagined the Worshipped is in a direction, up above, and that one can reach Him. Yet — Qushayrī notes — were He in a direction, one might conceive and permit reaching Him: to claim to climb up to God by a tower is to lower Him to the rank of localized things.

mots.ar: مَا عَلِمْتُ لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرِي
mots.tr: mā ʿalimtu lakum min ilāhin ghayrī
mots.racine: ع ل م / أ ل ه
mots.sens: I know no god for you but myself

mots.racine: و ق د / ط ي ن
mots.sens: kindle for me over the clay
climb up to the God of Moses

versets.ar: وَاسْتَكْبَرَ هُوَ وَجُنُودُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا يُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-stakbara huwa wa-junūduhu fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi wa-ẓannū annahum ilaynā lā yurjaʿūna
versets.trad: He and his hosts grew arrogant in the land without right, and thought they would never be returned to Us.
versets.commentaire: The root of all tyranny: to believe one will not return. Qushayrī joins this verse to the next: he persisted in perpetuating his denial and obstinacy, so God drowned him in the sea as He had drowned his heart in the sea of unbelief. The arrogance that thinks itself without a morrow is already engulfed within: the outward punishment only makes visible the inner drowning.

mots.sens: they would not return to Us

versets.ar: فَأَخَذْنَاهُ وَجُنُودَهُ فَنَبَذْنَاهُمْ فِي الْيَمِّ ۖ فَانظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-akhadhnāhu wa-junūdahu fa-nabadhnāhum fī l-yammi fa-nẓur kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: So We seized him and his hosts and cast them into the sea. Behold, then, how was the end of the wrongdoers!
versets.commentaire: The fulfillment of the decree. Qushayrī: he refused all but to perpetuate his denial and his arrogance; so God drowned him in the sea as He had drowned his heart in the sea of unbelief. The same flood (al-yamm) where his mother had cast Moses to save him engulfs Pharaoh to destroy him: the water of deliverance for some is the water of perdition for others.

mots.racine: ن ب ذ / ي م م
mots.sens: We cast them into the sea

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ ۖ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnāhum aʾimmatan yadʿūna ilā n-nāri wa-yawma l-qiyāmati lā yunṣarūna
versets.trad: We made them leaders who call to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not be helped.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī corrects any misreading of the word “leaders”: it is not for their honor that He made them chiefs, but because of their ruin, placing them foremost in disgrace; they guided only to error, pointed only to the impossible. They poured upon their followers the darkness of their hearts. To preside over evil is to precede in punishment.

mots.racine: أ م م / د ع و

versets.ar: وَأَتْبَعْنَاهُمْ فِي هَٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا لَعْنَةً ۖ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ هُم مِّنَ الْمَقْبُوحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-atbaʿnāhum fī hādhihi d-dunyā laʿnatan wa-yawma l-qiyāmati hum mina l-maqbūḥīna
versets.trad: We pursued them in this world with a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be among the reviled.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds a cascade of estrangements: in this world they were kept far from His knowledge, and in the next far from His forgiveness; they passed from banishment to banishment, from rejection to distancing, from separation to burning. The curse is not first a penalty inflicted but a distance carved out — exile from the Face of God.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ل ع ن
mots.sens: We pursued them with a curse

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ مِن بَعْدِ مَا أَهْلَكْنَا الْقُرُونَ الْأُولَىٰ بَصَائِرَ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba min baʿdi mā ahlaknā l-qurūna l-ūlā baṣāʾira li-n-nāsi wa-hudan wa-raḥmatan laʿallahum yatadhakkarūna
versets.trad: We gave Moses the Book, after We had destroyed the earlier generations, as clear insights for people, and a guidance and a mercy, that they might take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives an unexpected allusion: dwellings become pleasant only when emptied of strangers; the sweetest abodes are those adorned by the absence of watchers. When God had destroyed Pharaoh and his people, made Israel inherit their goods, life grew sweet for them and the suns of happiness rose upon them — and it was then the Book came, insight (baṣāʾir), guidance and mercy for whoever reflects.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب
ه ل ك / ق ر ن

versets.ar: وَمَا كُنتَ بِجَانِبِ الْغَرْبِيِّ إِذْ قَضَيْنَا إِلَىٰ مُوسَى الْأَمْرَ وَمَا كُنتَ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kunta bi-jānibi l-gharbiyyi idh qaḍaynā ilā mūsā l-amra wa-mā kunta mina sh-shāhidīna
versets.trad: You were not on the western side when We decreed the command to Moses, nor were you among the witnesses.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the Prophet's absence the very proof of his veracity: you were not present to know it by sight; yet they saw that your account could not be belied by their own Book. And since you are unlettered, unable to read, your knowledge can only come from Our teaching, from what We unveiled to you. The Prophet's knowledge of what he did not witness attests that he holds it from God alone.

mots.racine: ج ن ب / غ ر ب
ق ض ي / أ م ر
mots.sens: when We decreed the command to Moses

versets.ar: وَلَٰكِنَّا أَنشَأْنَا قُرُونًا فَتَطَاوَلَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْعُمُرُ ۚ وَمَا كُنتَ ثَاوِيًا فِي أَهْلِ مَدْيَنَ تَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِنَا وَلَٰكِنَّا كُنَّا مُرْسِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lākinnā anshaʾnā qurūnan fa-taṭāwala ʿalayhimu l-ʿumuru wa-mā kunta thāwiyan fī ahli madyana tatlū ʿalayhim āyātinā wa-lākinnā kunnā mursilīna
versets.trad: But We brought forth generations, and their span grew long upon them. Nor were you a dweller among the people of Madyan, reciting to them Our verses; but it is We who were sending the messengers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what Moses recited to his people was the praise of our Prophet and the fair tribute to his community. The Prophet dwelt neither in Madyan nor at the Mount; his knowledge comes from the pure divine sending. The lesson: the chain of prophethood is one, and the revelation given to Muḥammad confirms that of Moses — not by human transmission, but by the decree of the Truth “who sends.”

mots.racine: ن ش أ / ق ر ن
ط و ل / ع م ر

versets.ar: وَمَا كُنتَ بِجَانِبِ الطُّورِ إِذْ نَادَيْنَا وَلَٰكِن رَّحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ لِتُنذِرَ قَوْمًا مَّا أَتَاهُم مِّن نَّذِيرٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kunta bi-jānibi ṭ-ṭūri idh nādaynā wa-lākin raḥmatan min rabbika li-tundhira qawman mā atāhum min nadhīrin min qablika laʿallahum yatadhakkarūna
versets.trad: You were not on the side of the Mount when We called out, but it is a mercy from your Lord, that you might warn a people to whom no warner had come before you, that they might take heed.
versets.commentaire: One of the sūra's pearls. Qushayrī: what Moses asked for his community, We have granted to yours; and as We called Moses while he was in existence and manifestation, We called you while you were still in hidden nonexistence. He cites the verse: “Be for me as You were when I was not.” The Prophet's mission is pure mercy, descended upon those whom no warner had visited.

mots.racine: ج ن ب / ط و ر
mots.sens: on the side of the Mount

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا أَن تُصِيبَهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُوا رَبَّنَا لَوْلَا أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ آيَاتِكَ وَنَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā an tuṣībahum muṣībatun bimā qaddamat aydīhim fa-yaqūlū rabbanā lawlā arsalta ilaynā rasūlan fa-nattabiʿa āyātika wa-nakūna mina l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: Were it not that a calamity might strike them for what their own hands have sent ahead, and they would then say: “Our Lord! Why did You not send us a messenger, that we might have followed Your signs and been among the believers?”
versets.commentaire: God forecloses every excuse in advance. Qushayrī: in the time of the interval (fatra), they wished God would send them a messenger to guide them, promising themselves faith and response. The sending of the Prophet comes to remove the pretext: none can say on the Day of Reckoning, “no one warned me.” Mercy precedes punishment so that there be no argument against justice.

mots.racine: ق د م / ي د ي
mots.sens: for what their hands sent ahead
why did You not send us a messenger

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوا لَوْلَا أُوتِيَ مِثْلَ مَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَىٰ ۚ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفُرُوا بِمَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ قَالُوا سِحْرَانِ تَظَاهَرَا وَقَالُوا إِنَّا بِكُلٍّ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāʾahumu l-ḥaqqu min ʿindinā qālū lawlā ūtiya mithla mā ūtiya mūsā a-wa-lam yakfurū bimā ūtiya mūsā min qablu qālū siḥrāni taẓāharā wa-qālū innā bi-kullin kāfirūna
versets.trad: But when the Truth came to them from Us, they said: “Why was he not given the like of what Moses was given?” Did they not reject what Moses was given before? They said: “Two kinds of magic supporting each other,” and they said: “We disbelieve in them all!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the caprice: in the time of the interval they promised faith; but when the messenger came they belied him, demanding that he be distinguished by miracles like Moses' — which was on their part an error, a demand out of place, a cavil after the excuse had been removed. Their heart seeks not proof but pretext: to one who wills to deny, all truth appears as magic.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ح ق ق

mots.ar: لَوْلَا أُوتِيَ مِثْلَ مَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَىٰ
mots.tr: lawlā ūtiya mithla mā ūtiya mūsā

mots.racine: س ح ر / ظ ه ر

versets.ar: قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِكِتَابٍ مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ هُوَ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْهُمَا أَتَّبِعْهُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul faʾtū bi-kitābin min ʿindi llāhi huwa ahdā minhumā attabiʿhu in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Say: “Then bring a Book from God that is a better guide than these two, and I will follow it, if you are truthful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī comments on the challenge: if you doubt that this Book is from God, bring one like it, and call your associates to your aid — then he concludes, certain: from his time to ours, none has produced a single sūra like it, and until the Resurrection none will produce a Book like it. The inimitability of the Qurʾān is itself the sign the contestants vainly demanded.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب

mots.sens: a better guide than the two

versets.ar: فَإِن لَّمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَكَ فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّمَا يَتَّبِعُونَ أَهْوَاءَهُمْ ۚ وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in lam yastajībū laka fa-ʿlam annamā yattabiʿūna ahwāʾahum wa-man aḍallu mimmani ttabaʿa hawāhu bi-ghayri hudan mina llāhi inna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: If they do not answer you, know that they only follow their own desires. And who is more astray than one who follows his desire without guidance from God? Indeed, God does not guide the wrongdoing people.
versets.commentaire: The deepest misguidance, says the verse, is to follow one's desire without guidance. In Qushayrī's spirit, the hawā (one's own craving) is the veil between the soul and the truth: whoever makes it his compass turns in circles in the valleys of his own ruin. Guidance is not won by argument but received from God — and He does not grant it to the heart that has surrendered itself to wrongdoing.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه و ي

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ وَصَّلْنَا لَهُمُ الْقَوْلَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad waṣṣalnā lahumu l-qawla laʿallahum yatadhakkarūna
versets.trad: We have indeed conveyed to them the Word continuously, that they might take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We sent messenger after messenger, We made Book succeed Book — and they only increased in unbelief and ruin, in denial and rebellion; they returned neither to the Truth nor to uprightness. God's Word is ceaselessly stretched toward the heart of man; refusal is no fault of the call but the hardening of one who does not listen.

mots.racine: و ص ل / ق و ل
mots.sens: We conveyed the Word to them continuously
mots.def: waṣṣala: to make reach without break.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلِهِ هُم بِهِ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba min qablihi hum bihi yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Those to whom We gave the Book before it believe in it.
themes: the faith of the people of the Book
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes faith a gift of illumination: those whose insight We anointed with the light of guidance believed by Our solicitude; and the one We blinded to the contemplation of the Truth, whom the subtleties of divine assistance did not aid, sank into his misguidance. The recognition of the Qurʾān by the sincere of the earlier Scriptures proceeds from this inner light God grants.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَإِذَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ قَالُوا آمَنَّا بِهِ إِنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّنَا إِنَّا كُنَّا مِن قَبْلِهِ مُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā yutlā ʿalayhim qālū āmannā bihi innahu l-ḥaqqu min rabbinā innā kunnā min qablihi muslimīna
versets.trad: When it is recited to them, they say: “We believe in it; it is the Truth from our Lord; even before it we were submitters (muslimūn).”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they hear Our call, they meet it with assent and submit in fair surrender. Their “we were already submitters” bespeaks a disposition prior to the letter: the prepared heart recognizes the Truth as soon as it hears it, because it was secretly granted to it before ever encountering it.

mots.sens: when it is recited to them
it is the Truth from our Lord

mots.def: muslim: one who yields to God.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ يُؤْتَوْنَ أَجْرَهُم مَّرَّتَيْنِ بِمَا صَبَرُوا وَيَدْرَءُونَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika yuʾtawna ajrahum marratayni bimā ṣabarū wa-yadraʾūna bi-l-ḥasanati s-sayyiʾata wa-mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūna
versets.trad: Those will be given their reward twice over, for they were patient, and they repel evil with good, and they spend out of what We have provided them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because they heard Our call and answered it with assent and fair submission, they are inevitably given their wage twice: for being patient in fulfilling the commands and patient before the prohibitions — once in the next world, which is the recompense, and once in this world, which is the subtle graces of nearness. Patience that returns good for evil is doubly paid.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / م ر ر
د ر أ / ح س ن

versets.ar: وَإِذَا سَمِعُوا اللَّغْوَ أَعْرَضُوا عَنْهُ وَقَالُوا لَنَا أَعْمَالُنَا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمَالُكُمْ سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ لَا نَبْتَغِي الْجَاهِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā samiʿū l-laghwa aʿraḍū ʿanhu wa-qālū lanā aʿmālunā wa-lakum aʿmālukum salāmun ʿalaykum lā nabtaghī l-jāhilīna
versets.trad: When they hear idle talk, they turn away from it and say: “To us our deeds and to you your deeds. Peace be upon you! We do not seek the ignorant.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the “idle” (laghw) by a series of flashes: what turns one from God; what affords no means of approach to Him; what is neither by the Truth nor for the Truth; what issues from a heedless heart; what, heard, brings forgetfulness. To turn from it with a peaceable greeting is to keep the heart free for the sole remembrance of God.

mots.racine: س م ع / ل غ و
ب غ ي / ج ه ل
mots.sens: we do not seek the ignorant

versets.ar: إِنَّكَ لَا تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: innaka lā tahdī man aḥbabta wa-lākinna llāha yahdī man yashāʾu wa-huwa aʿlamu bi-l-muhtadīna
versets.trad: You cannot guide whom you love, but God guides whom He wills, and He knows best those who are guided.
versets.commentaire: A pivotal verse. Qushayrī: guidance, in reality, is the inclining of the heart from the false to the True, and this belongs to the exclusive properties of the Truth's power. To the Prophet belongs the call — “and indeed you guide to a straight path” — but the effusion of faith into the heart is God's alone. And he adds: you have the honor of prophethood, the rank of envoyship, the beauty of intercession… yet you cannot guide whom you love, for the prerogatives of Lordship do not befit one described by humanity.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ح ب ب
mots.sens: you cannot guide whom you love

mots.racine: ه د ي / ش ي أ
ع ل م / ه د ي

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا إِن نَّتَّبِعِ الْهُدَىٰ مَعَكَ نُتَخَطَّفْ مِنْ أَرْضِنَا ۚ أَوَلَمْ نُمَكِّن لَّهُمْ حَرَمًا آمِنًا يُجْبَىٰ إِلَيْهِ ثَمَرَاتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ رِّزْقًا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū in nattabiʿi l-hudā maʿaka nutakhaṭṭaf min arḍinā a-wa-lam numakkin lahum ḥaraman āminan yujbā ilayhi thamarātu kulli shayʾin rizqan min ladunnā wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: They say: “If we followed the guidance with you, we would be snatched from our land.” Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary, to which the produce of all things is brought, a provision from Us? But most of them do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the verse into a spiritual teaching: whoever fulfills the right of God, the whole universe is made subject to him; whoever guards his secret for God, fulfills God's right, and spends his moments in God's worship, is granted power to act within God's kingdom — creation is subject to him, time obeys his command, and the Truth takes charge of his days and works, verifies his thought and does not waste his right. To fear being “snatched away” for following guidance is to ignore that guidance itself is the surest refuge.

mots.sens: we would be snatched from our land

mots.racine: ح ر م / أ م ن
ج ب ي / ث م ر
mots.sens: the fruits of all things are brought to it

versets.ar: وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ بَطِرَتْ مَعِيشَتَهَا ۖ فَتِلْكَ مَسَاكِنُهُمْ لَمْ تُسْكَن مِّن بَعْدِهِمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا ۖ وَكُنَّا نَحْنُ الْوَارِثِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kam ahlaknā min qaryatin baṭirat maʿīshatahā fa-tilka masākinuhum lam tuskan min baʿdihim illā qalīlan wa-kunnā naḥnu l-wārithīna
versets.trad: How many a town have We destroyed that exulted insolently in its livelihood! There are their dwellings, scarcely inhabited after them. And it is We who were the inheritors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they did not know the worth of their grace, gave no thanks for the safety of their states nor the order of their affairs; they wandered blindly in the valleys of ingratitude, and God made them taste from the cups of humiliation what shattered the intoxication of their insolence. Their houses stand empty, the roofs fallen in upon them, “the crows of ruin cawing over them.” Opulence without gratitude prepares desolation.

mots.racine: ب ط ر / ع ي ش
mots.def: baṭira: to be insolent in plenty.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ الْقُرَىٰ حَتَّىٰ يَبْعَثَ فِي أُمِّهَا رَسُولًا يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِنَا ۚ وَمَا كُنَّا مُهْلِكِي الْقُرَىٰ إِلَّا وَأَهْلُهَا ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna rabbuka muhlika l-qurā ḥattā yabʿatha fī ummihā rasūlan yatlū ʿalayhim āyātinā wa-mā kunnā muhlikī l-qurā illā wa-ahluhā ẓālimūna
versets.trad: Your Lord would never destroy the towns until He raised in their mother-city a messenger reciting to them Our verses. Nor would We destroy the towns unless their people were wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He commands them by prescription (taklīf) and halts them by the command of engendering (takwīn) as He wills; He sends the messengers as warning and veils the paths to them by sheer power; He makes the proof so plain that no doubt remains — yet He guides only the one for whom felicity was assigned by the decree of apportionment. Destruction strikes only after warning and only injustice: divine justice surprises no one.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ي
ب ع ث / أ م م
mots.sens: raise in their mother-city a messenger

versets.ar: وَمَا أُوتِيتُم مِّن شَيْءٍ فَمَتَاعُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتُهَا ۚ وَمَا عِندَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā ūtītum min shayʾin fa-matāʿu l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-zīnatuhā wa-mā ʿinda llāhi khayrun wa-abqā a-fa-lā taʿqilūna
versets.trad: Whatever you have been given is but the enjoyment and adornment of the life of this world. What is with God is better and more lasting. Will you not use reason?
versets.commentaire: A pearl of Qushayrī: the world is sweet and verdant, but in reality bitter and spoiled; its glitter makes it seem pure, but behind its purity is a turbid draught — and what is with God is better and more lasting. The verdict of reason is here invoked: whoever weighs the passing against the abiding cannot hesitate. The world's adornment is a reflection; God's abode, a substance.

mots.racine: م ت ع / د ن و
mots.sens: enjoyment of the life of this world

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ب ق ي

versets.ar: أَفَمَن وَعَدْنَاهُ وَعْدًا حَسَنًا فَهُوَ لَاقِيهِ كَمَن مَّتَّعْنَاهُ مَتَاعَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ هُوَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مِنَ الْمُحْضَرِينَ
versets.tr: a-fa-man waʿadnāhu waʿdan ḥasanan fa-huwa lāqīhi ka-man mattaʿnāhu matāʿa l-ḥayāti d-dunyā thumma huwa yawma l-qiyāmati mina l-muḥḍarīna
versets.trad: Is then one to whom We have made a fair promise, which he will meet, like one whom We have given the enjoyment of the life of this world, and who then, on the Day of Resurrection, will be among those brought forth?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two fates with a striking image: the world is a poison whose colocynth-bitterness follows the taste of its honey; the loss its draught brings outweighs the sweetness its surface shows. And he concludes: one honored with the enjoyment of the next world's delights is not like one tried by the fall into the hellfire of his this-world. The fair promise fulfilled bears no comparison with the enjoyment that ends in being haled forth.

mots.racine: و ع د / ح س ن
mots.sens: We made him a fair promise

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكَائِيَ الَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yunādīhim fa-yaqūlu ayna shurakāʾiya lladhīna kuntum tazʿumūna
versets.trad: On the Day He will call to them and say: “Where are My associates whom you used to claim?”
versets.commentaire: This call, says Qushayrī, is to terrify and to nullify the cunning of the misguided — otherwise, whence would they have a reply, let alone a right one? And he recalls the root of all things: He who questions them is He who made them as He willed; no act has arisen save from His act, no effect issued save from His origin. The divine question awaits no information but unveils the nothingness of the idols.

mots.sens: the Day He calls to them

versets.ar: قَالَ الَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلُ رَبَّنَا هَٰؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَغْوَيْنَا أَغْوَيْنَاهُمْ كَمَا غَوَيْنَا ۖ تَبَرَّأْنَا إِلَيْكَ ۖ مَا كَانُوا إِيَّانَا يَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: qāla lladhīna ḥaqqa ʿalayhimu l-qawlu rabbanā hāʾulāʾi lladhīna aghwaynā aghwaynāhum kamā ghawaynā tabarraʾnā ilayka mā kānū iyyānā yaʿbudūna
versets.trad: Those against whom the sentence is proved will say: “Our Lord! These are the ones we led astray. We led them astray as we ourselves were astray. We disavow them before You; it was not us they worshipped.”
versets.commentaire: When some disavow others, says Qushayrī, it becomes manifest that the idols had no claim to worship, and that no one, in negation or affirmation, has the least share in the power to bring into being. All falls back upon the One: no — He is the One, the Subduer (al-Wāḥid al-Qahhār). The seducers' disavowal on the Last Day reveals the vanity of all that was worshipped beside God.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ق و ل
mots.sens: the sentence is proved against them
we led them astray as we strayed

versets.ar: وَقِيلَ ادْعُوا شُرَكَاءَكُمْ فَدَعَوْهُمْ فَلَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَهُمْ وَرَأَوُا الْعَذَابَ ۚ لَوْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qīla dʿū shurakāʾakum fa-daʿawhum fa-lam yastajībū lahum wa-raʾawu l-ʿadhāba law annahum kānū yahtadūna
versets.trad: It will be said: “Call upon your associates!” So they will call them, but they will not answer them, and they will see the punishment. If only they had been guided!
versets.commentaire: The verse stages the total failure of the false deities: called at the moment of extreme need, they remain mute, and the punishment appears. In Qushayrī's spirit, this silence is the sentence itself: what has neither power of existence nor power of help cannot answer. The lament “if only they had been guided” bespeaks the bitterness of one who discovers too late that his recourse was mere nothingness.

mots.racine: د ع و / ش ر ك
ر أ ي / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَا أَجَبْتُمُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yunādīhim fa-yaqūlu mādhā ajabtumu l-mursalīna
versets.trad: On the Day He will call to them and say: “What did you answer the messengers?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He questions them with a questioning that inspires awe (hayba), so that there remains in them neither discernment, nor strength of mind, nor capacity to answer. The question “what did you answer the messengers?” is not a demand for confession but sacred terror: before the majesty of the One who questions, the tongue is bound and reason is effaced.

versets.ar: فَعَمِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَنبَاءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَهُمْ لَا يَتَسَاءَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ʿamiyat ʿalayhimu l-anbāʾu yawmaʾidhin fa-hum lā yatasāʾalūna
versets.trad: The tidings will be obscured to them on that Day, and they will not even question one another.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: perplexity seizes them, bewilderment overcomes them — no speech, no reason, no discernment, no understanding. The “tidings” that served them as arguments are extinguished before their eyes; they are so struck with stupor that they can no longer even question one another. The Day of truth dissolves all false certainties.

mots.racine: ع م ي / ن ب أ
mots.sens: the tidings were obscured to them
they do not question one another

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا مَن تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَعَسَىٰ أَن يَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُفْلِحِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ammā man tāba wa-āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-ʿasā an yakūna mina l-mufliḥīna
versets.trad: As for one who repents, believes and does good, he may perhaps be among the successful.
versets.commentaire: At the heart of the Day's terror, a door of hope: repentance joined to faith and good works. In Qushayrī's perspective, the “perhaps” (ʿasā) coming from God is not uncertainty but a veiled promise of gentleness: whoever returns to Him through the heart's conversion, through trust and upright action, enters the company of the successful. The triad tawba-īmān-ʿamal traces the way of salvation.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح

versets.ar: وَرَبُّكَ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَاءُ وَيَخْتَارُ ۗ مَا كَانَ لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ ۚ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَتَعَالَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-rabbuka yakhluqu mā yashāʾu wa-yakhtāru mā kāna lahumu l-khiyaratu subḥāna llāhi wa-taʿālā ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: Your Lord creates what He wills and chooses; theirs is not the choice. Glory be to God! Exalted is He above what they associate with Him.
versets.commentaire: One of Qushayrī's great pages: choice belongs to the Truth by a right of majesty requiring that it be His; for were He not to enact His will and choice, He would not be described by power. And: choice, for the servant, is not blessed, for it is an attribute he has no right to; whoever adorns himself with what does not befit him dishonors himself. The servant's peace is to efface himself before the divine election: what has man to do with choosing?

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ش ي أ

versets.ar: وَرَبُّكَ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-rabbuka yaʿlamu mā tukinnu ṣudūruhum wa-mā yuʿlinūna
versets.trad: Your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they disclose.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: and how should He not know it, He who said: “Does He who created not know, He the Subtle, the Well-Informed?” The knowledge from which no object is hidden is an attribute of Him who is without beginning; and the bringing-forth from nonexistence into existence belongs to Him alone, eternally. God knows the secret of the breasts before it even forms within them.

mots.racine: ك ن ن / ص د ر

versets.ar: وَهُوَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ لَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي الْأُولَىٰ وَالْآخِرَةِ ۖ وَلَهُ الْحُكْمُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa llāhu lā ilāha illā huwa lahu l-ḥamdu fī l-ūlā wa-l-ākhirati wa-lahu l-ḥukmu wa-ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: He is God; there is no god but He! To Him is praise in the first life and the last! To Him belongs the judgment, and to Him you will be returned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī is illumined by the formula of oneness: “there is no god but He” — He singled Himself out by the majesty of His awe, set Himself apart by the glory of His Lordship; no like equals Him, no peer resembles Him. And: to Him is praise, in right of His giving; to Him is gratitude, by reason of His grace — in this world the Praised is God, in the next the Thanked is God. Bounty, grace, help: all is from Him, for to Him belong the authority and the power.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِن جَعَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمُ اللَّيْلَ سَرْمَدًا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ مَنْ إِلَٰهٌ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِضِيَاءٍ ۖ أَفَلَا تَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum in jaʿala llāhu ʿalaykumu l-layla sarmadan ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati man ilāhun ghayru llāhi yaʾtīkum bi-ḍiyāʾin a-fa-lā tasmaʿūna
versets.trad: Say: “Have you considered, if God should make the night perpetual over you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than God could bring you light? Will you not hear?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the cosmic verse into an inner journey, in a cascade of images: if the night of the interval lasted, who would bring the day of repentance but God? If the night of the quest lasted, who would bring the dawn of finding but God? If the night of contraction (qabḍ) lasted, who would bring the morning of expansion but God? If the night of separation lasted, who would bring the dawn of union but God? Light, outer or inner, is His gift alone.

mots.racine: ل ي ل / س ر م د
أ ت ي / ض و أ

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِن جَعَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمُ النَّهَارَ سَرْمَدًا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ مَنْ إِلَٰهٌ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِلَيْلٍ تَسْكُنُونَ فِيهِ ۖ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum in jaʿala llāhu ʿalaykumu n-nahāra sarmadan ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati man ilāhun ghayru llāhi yaʾtīkum bi-laylin taskunūna fīhi a-fa-lā tubṣirūna
versets.trad: Say: “Have you considered, if God should make the day perpetual over you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than God could bring you a night in which to rest? Will you not see?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues the allegory: if the day of your union lasted, what way would the slanderers have to spoil your joy? And if the day of your subsistence and busyness with your portions lasted, who other than God would bring you a night in which to rest in God, to find ease from your occupations through seclusion with God, but God? Rest itself is not in the night but in intimacy with the One.

mots.racine: ن ه ر / س ر م د
ل ي ل / س ك ن

versets.ar: وَمِن رَّحْمَتِهِ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِن فَضْلِهِ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min raḥmatihi jaʿala lakumu l-layla wa-n-nahāra li-taskunū fīhi wa-li-tabtaghū min faḍlihi wa-laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: Out of His mercy He made for you the night and the day, that you may rest in it and seek of His bounty, and that you may give thanks.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates the relativity of times according to the heart's states: the hours are vessels for what occurs in them; time in itself is homogeneous, and the difference lies in what fills it — the nights of the people of union are the princesses of nights, those of the people of separation the worst. For the near, nights and days are short; for the distant, they stretch long. The night of rest and the day of seeking are one and the same mercy, to be received with gratitude.

mots.sens: that you may rest in it

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ف ض ل

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكَائِيَ الَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yunādīhim fa-yaqūlu ayna shurakāʾiya lladhīna kuntum tazʿumūna
versets.trad: On the Day He will call to them and say: “Where are My associates whom you used to claim?”
versets.commentaire: The question is repeated to drive home the failure of the idols. In Qushayrī's perspective, this repetition is no redundancy but a deepening dismay: at each call, the absence of an answer hollows out further the confusion of the associators. Where they placed partners to God, they find only emptiness — and the emptiness itself is their sentence.

mots.sens: the Day He calls to them

versets.ar: وَنَزَعْنَا مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا فَقُلْنَا هَاتُوا بُرْهَانَكُمْ فَعَلِمُوا أَنَّ الْحَقَّ لِلَّهِ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-nazaʿnā min kulli ummatin shahīdan fa-qulnā hātū burhānakum fa-ʿalimū anna l-ḥaqqa li-llāhi wa-ḍalla ʿanhum mā kānū yaftarūna
versets.trad: We shall draw from every community a witness and say: “Bring your proof!” Then they will know that the Truth belongs to God, and what they invented will forsake them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: no — they have no argument, no answer to excuse them, no intercessor to pity them, no ally to help them; their misguidance has become notorious, their ignorance plain to all. When their “proof” is demanded, it slips away, and they at last acknowledge that the Truth belongs to God alone: all they forged vanishes.

mots.racine: ن ز ع / ش ه د
mots.sens: We drew from every community a witness

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ف ر ي

versets.ar: إِنَّ قَارُونَ كَانَ مِن قَوْمِ مُوسَىٰ فَبَغَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ وَآتَيْنَاهُ مِنَ الْكُنُوزِ مَا إِنَّ مَفَاتِحَهُ لَتَنُوءُ بِالْعُصْبَةِ أُولِي الْقُوَّةِ إِذْ قَالَ لَهُ قَوْمُهُ لَا تَفْرَحْ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْفَرِحِينَ
versets.tr: inna qārūna kāna min qawmi mūsā fa-baghā ʿalayhim wa-ātaynāhu mina l-kunūzi mā inna mafātiḥahu la-tanūʾu bi-l-ʿuṣbati ulī l-quwwati idh qāla lahu qawmuhu lā tafraḥ inna llāha lā yuḥibbu l-fariḥīna
versets.trad: Korah (Qārūn) was of the people of Moses, but he wronged them. We had given him such treasures that their very keys would weigh down a band of strong men. His people said to him: “Do not exult! God does not love the exultant!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī tells the story of Qārūn, at first one of the most devout of the Israelites, secluded in his hermitage. Satan, disguised, drew him by degrees into hoarding: love of the world led him to amass, amassing to love it, and that love to arrogance against his own — the abundance of his wealth became the cause of his ruin. “Do not exult, God does not love the exultant”: the joy rooted in possession, and not in the Giver, is already the seed of perdition.

mots.sens: he was of the people of Moses

mots.racine: ف ت ح / ن و أ
mots.sens: its keys weighed down a band

versets.ar: وَابْتَغِ فِيمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ ۖ وَلَا تَنسَ نَصِيبَكَ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَأَحْسِن كَمَا أَحْسَنَ اللَّهُ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَلَا تَبْغِ الْفَسَادَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-btaghi fīmā ātāka llāhu d-dāra l-ākhirata wa-lā tansa naṣībaka mina d-dunyā wa-aḥsin kamā aḥsana llāhu ilayka wa-lā tabghi l-fasāda fī l-arḍi inna llāha lā yuḥibbu l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: Seek, through what God has given you, the abode of the Hereafter, and do not forget your share of this world. Do good as God has done good to you, and do not seek corruption in the land. God does not love the corrupters.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī redefines the “share” of the world: the share of this world is neither amassing it nor withholding it; it is what bears a benefit without leaving regret or entailing punishment in the next life. And higher still: the share of this world is what drives one to obey Him with the body, to know Him with the heart, to invoke Him with the tongue, to contemplate Him with the secret. As for the good: iḥsān is to see the grace without imagining one has a right to it.

mots.ar: وَابْتَغِ فِيمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ
mots.tr: wa-btaghi fīmā ātāka llāhu d-dāra l-ākhira
mots.racine: ب غ ي / د و ر
mots.sens: seek the Hereafter through your gifts

mots.racine: ن س ي / ن ص ب
mots.sens: do not forget your share of this world
do good as God did to you

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّمَا أُوتِيتُهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ عِندِي ۚ أَوَلَمْ يَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَهْلَكَ مِن قَبْلِهِ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ مَنْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُ قُوَّةً وَأَكْثَرُ جَمْعًا ۚ وَلَا يُسْأَلُ عَن ذُنُوبِهِمُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla innamā ūtītuhu ʿalā ʿilmin ʿindī a-wa-lam yaʿlam anna llāha qad ahlaka min qablihi mina l-qurūni man huwa ashaddu minhu quwwatan wa-aktharu jamʿan wa-lā yusʾalu ʿan dhunūbihimu l-mujrimūna
versets.trad: He said: “I was given it only because of knowledge I possess.” Did he not know that God had destroyed before him, among the generations, those mightier than him in strength and greater in accumulation? But the criminals are not questioned about their sins.
versets.commentaire: Qārūn's words “I owe it to my knowledge” are the height of illusion. Qushayrī: no one has regarded himself without perishing through his own conceit (ʿujb). And: the killing poison, that which extinguishes the shining lamp, is to cast upon oneself a gaze of affirmation, and to imagine there is in oneself a power to negate or affirm. To attribute the gift to one's merit is to veil oneself from the Giver — and that veil is the root of ruin.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م
mots.sens: I was given it through my knowledge

mots.racine: ش د د / ق و ي
س أ ل / ذ ن ب
mots.sens: they are not questioned about their sins

versets.ar: فَخَرَجَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ فِي زِينَتِهِ ۖ قَالَ الَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا يَا لَيْتَ لَنَا مِثْلَ مَا أُوتِيَ قَارُونُ إِنَّهُ لَذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: fa-kharaja ʿalā qawmihi fī zīnatihi qāla lladhīna yurīdūna l-ḥayāta d-dunyā yā layta lanā mithla mā ūtiya qārūnu innahu la-dhū ḥaẓẓin ʿaẓīmin
versets.trad: Then he went forth to his people in his finery. Those who desired the life of this world said: “Would that we had the like of what Korah has been given! He has truly an immense fortune!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who saw him — they whom love of the world set on his level — wished God would give them the like of what He had given him. Qārūn's splendor dazzles only eyes mired in this world. Outward pomp is a mirror in which each reads his own desire: whoever wants the world envies the opulence; whoever wants God sees its vanity (next verse).

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ز ي ن
mots.sens: he went forth in his finery

mots.ar: يَا لَيْتَ لَنَا مِثْلَ مَا أُوتِيَ قَارُونُ
mots.tr: yā layta lanā mithla mā ūtiya qārūn
mots.sens: would that we had like Korah

mots.racine: ح ظ ظ / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَيْلَكُمْ ثَوَابُ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِّمَنْ آمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا وَلَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا الصَّابِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma waylakum thawābu llāhi khayrun li-man āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan wa-lā yulaqqāhā illā ṣ-ṣābirūna
versets.trad: But those who had been given knowledge said: “Woe to you! The reward of God is better for one who believes and does good, and none will receive it except the patient.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts the two gazes: as for those sobered from the intoxication of heedlessness, awakened by the light of their insight, their stance was: “The reward of God is better.” The “people of knowledge” are not the scholars of letters but those whose heart sees beyond the surface; and that vision — like the reward it promises — is granted only to the patient (al-ṣābirūn).

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م
ث و ب / خ ي ر
ل ق ي / ص ب ر
mots.sens: none receive it but the patient

versets.ar: فَخَسَفْنَا بِهِ وَبِدَارِهِ الْأَرْضَ فَمَا كَانَ لَهُ مِن فِئَةٍ يَنصُرُونَهُ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُنتَصِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-khasafnā bihi wa-bi-dārihi l-arḍa fa-mā kāna lahu min fiʾatin yanṣurūnahu min dūni llāhi wa-mā kāna mina l-muntaṣirīna
versets.trad: So We caused the earth to swallow him and his house. He had no host to help him against God, nor was he of those who could defend themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī relates the tradition: God swallowed him through the prayer of Moses, who said: “O earth, take him!”; and as the earth was swallowing him, Qārūn implored Moses by the bond of kinship — but Moses kept repeating: “O earth, take him.” Then God addressed to Moses this poignant reproach: “He called upon you by kinship and you said: O earth, take him! I say: O servant, call upon Me, I am nearer to you than he — but he did not say it.” Whoever has made himself deaf to God his whole life no longer knows how to call upon Him in the hour of his fall.

mots.racine: خ س ف / د و ر
mots.sens: We made the earth swallow him and his house

mots.racine: ف أ ي / ن ص ر

versets.ar: وَأَصْبَحَ الَّذِينَ تَمَنَّوْا مَكَانَهُ بِالْأَمْسِ يَقُولُونَ وَيْكَأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ ۖ لَوْلَا أَن مَّنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا لَخَسَفَ بِنَا ۖ وَيْكَأَنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aṣbaḥa lladhīna tamannaw makānahu bi-l-amsi yaqūlūna wayka-anna llāha yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-yaqdiru lawlā an manna llāhu ʿalaynā la-khasafa binā wayka-annahu lā yufliḥu l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: And those who had wished to be in his place the day before began to say: “How true it is that God expands provision for whom He wills of His servants, and restricts it! Had God not been gracious to us, He would have caused the earth to swallow us. How true that the disbelievers do not prosper!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts the two outcomes: those who had envied his place repented, and those content with the divine apportionment were saved. And the words “had God not been gracious to us” become a praise: God favored us, so that we did not set out on his road nor enlist in his file — else ruin would have struck us. The covetous regret; the content are saved here below, to shine forth tomorrow.

mots.racine: م ن ي / ك و ن
ب س ط / ر ز ق

versets.ar: تِلْكَ الدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ نَجْعَلُهَا لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُرِيدُونَ عُلُوًّا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فَسَادًا ۚ وَالْعَاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: tilka d-dāru l-ākhiratu najʿaluhā li-lladhīna lā yurīdūna ʿuluwwan fī l-arḍi wa-lā fasādan wa-l-ʿāqibatu li-l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: That abode of the Hereafter We assign to those who desire neither exaltation in the land nor corruption. And the happy outcome is for the godfearing.
