versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a reading from the heights: “exaltation on earth,” for the great ones, is to imagine there exists on earth anyone worse than you; and “corruption” is to stir for your own portion, even by a breath or a step. Then, for the common: not to seek to rise like Pharaoh, nor to corrupt like Qārūn. And he distinguishes: the ascetics desire no exaltation on earth; the knowers desire none even in Paradise.

mots.racine: د و ر / أ خ ر
ع ق ب / و ق ي
mots.sens: the outcome is for the godfearing

versets.ar: مَن جَاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهَا ۖ وَمَن جَاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَا يُجْزَى الَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السَّيِّئَاتِ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: man jāʾa bi-l-ḥasanati fa-lahu khayrun minhā wa-man jāʾa bi-s-sayyiʾati fa-lā yujzā lladhīna ʿamilū s-sayyiʾāti illā mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: Whoever comes with a good deed shall have better than it. And whoever comes with an evil deed — those who do evil deeds shall be requited only for what they did.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī highlights the divine generosity: the reward of the good deed is in multiplication, and the treatment of the evil one is founded on lightening. The good is paid beyond its measure, the evil only to its just measure. And he adds for the believer: even laden with great faults, his sins are short beside his good works — which are his faith and his knowledge.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ح س ن
mots.sens: whoever comes with a good deed

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِي فَرَضَ عَلَيْكَ الْقُرْآنَ لَرَادُّكَ إِلَىٰ مَعَادٍ ۚ قُل رَّبِّي أَعْلَمُ مَن جَاءَ بِالْهُدَىٰ وَمَنْ هُوَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: inna lladhī faraḍa ʿalayka l-qurʾāna la-rādduka ilā maʿādin qul rabbī aʿlamu man jāʾa bi-l-hudā wa-man huwa fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: Indeed, He who has prescribed the Qurʾān for you will surely return you to a place of return. Say: “My Lord knows best who comes with guidance and who is in manifest error.”
versets.commentaire: A verse of manifold depths in Qushayrī: “will return you to a place of return (maʿād)” — outwardly, to Mecca, for he often repeated “the homeland, the homeland!”, and God granted his request. But by allusion: the maʿād is the state in which your spirit was before the wounds of love, amid the caresses of nearness and the contemplations of the Truth. And again: He who set you up in the attributes of dispersion by transmission will return you to the source of union, through realization in the Truth and extinction to the creature.

mots.racine: ف ر ض / ق ر أ
ر د د / ع و د
mots.sens: will return you to a place of return

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَمَا كُنتَ تَرْجُو أَن يُلْقَىٰ إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابُ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ ظَهِيرًا لِّلْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kunta tarjū an yulqā ilayka l-kitābu illā raḥmatan min rabbika fa-lā takūnanna ẓahīran li-l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: You did not hope that the Book would be cast to you; it was only a mercy from your Lord. So do not be a supporter of the disbelievers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: you did not hope for the rank of prophethood, the honor of envoyship, the fitness to receive Our address — nor what We unveiled to you of the states of ecstasy and the realities of oneness. The Book is not the fruit of a merited waiting but pure mercy descended without prior cause. Before such a gratuitous gift, the only response is never to back those who reject it.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ك ت ب
mots.sens: that the Book be cast to you
but a mercy from your Lord

versets.ar: وَلَا يَصُدُّنَّكَ عَنْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أُنزِلَتْ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَادْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaṣuddunnaka ʿan āyāti llāhi baʿda idh unzilat ilayka wa-dʿu ilā rabbika wa-lā takūnanna mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Let them not turn you away from the verses of God after they have been sent down to you. Call to your Lord, and do not be of those who associate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: let nothing, after their descent upon you, turn you from what you have found through fusion and contemplation, through grasping and finding. And: let no suspicion of cavils penetrate you, nor the objections of the learned who invoke the rulings of reason; for what is perceived in the blaze of the sun, its fading in the glimmer of a lamp cannot declare false. The certainty of unveiling is not to be shaken by quibbles.

mots.sens: let them not turn you away
after they were sent down to you

versets.ar: وَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ ۘ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ ۚ لَهُ الْحُكْمُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tadʿu maʿa llāhi ilāhan ākhara lā ilāha illā huwa kullu shayʾin hālikun illā wajhahu lahu l-ḥukmu wa-ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: And do not call upon another god besides God. There is no god but He! Everything is perishing except His Face. To Him belongs the judgment, and to Him you will be returned.
versets.commentaire: The sublime seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: every work is vain save what was for the Face of God and to draw near to Him; every living thing is dead save He — everything is destined to the possibility of annihilation and nonexistence, and there remains only “His Face.” And he scrutinizes the word “Face”: it is one of His attributes, subsisting only by Him; in the permanence of His Face is the permanence of His Essence and His attributes. All passes, save the Face of the Living who does not die — and toward Him all returns.

mots.ar: وَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ
mots.tr: wa-lā tadʿu maʿa llāhi ilāhan ākhar
mots.racine: د ع و / أ ل ه
mots.sens: do not call another god besides God
to Him you will be returned
